So this financial ratio makes it easy for investors to compare the relative profitability of multiple companies in the same industry. This article in our series on financial key ratios discusses the enterprise value-based multiples EV/EBITDA, EV/EBITA, EV/EBIT, and EV/Sales. That is why a company can trade at ~10x on an EV/EBITDA basis yet at a much richer ~18x on EV/EBIT once depreciation and amortization are recognized. **EV/EBIT:** Use this ratio when you want a read on recurring operating profitability that is capital structure neutral and still reflects the economic use of long-lived assets. The EV/Sales multiple helps investors measure value when profits are thin or negative since it compares the value of the whole company to revenue and bypasses earnings that can be below zero.","score":0.26416063,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.79,"request_id":"5e579c5f-9701-4c2d-92a5-65af45cacaf7"},"get_key_ratios_UCO":{"query":"UCO key financial ratios P/E EV/EBITDA profit margins ROE return on equity 2024 2025","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.smart-investing.in/financial-ratios.php?Company=UCO+BANK&p=ROE","title":"Return on Equity (ROE) of UCO BANK-Mar2025 - Smart-Investing.in","content":"The latest Return on Equity (ROE) ratio of UCO BANK is 9.39 based on Mar2025 Consolidated results.
